title: **Plans for Major Transformation of Iconic Rochdale Mosque**
excerpt:
**Plans for Major Transformation of Iconic Rochdale Mosque**

Rochdale is set to witness significant changes as plans for a new mosque in the town centre are unveiled. The proposed project aims to tra...
content_html:
**Plans for Major Transformation of Iconic Rochdale Mosque**

Rochdale is set to witness significant changes as plans for a new mosque in the town centre are unveiled. The proposed project aims to transform one of the area’s most recognised religious landmarks into a modern facility, described as a ‘legacy project’ for the community.

Details about the design and funding of the new mosque have yet to be fully disclosed, but local sources indicate that the initiative has garnered support within the community.

As the conversation surrounding the project unfolds, it has sparked various reactions from residents, with discussions focusing on the balance between new developments and existing places of worship.

Further updates on the project are expected as plans progress.

title: **Concerns Raised Over Mosque Construction and Church Closures in Rochdale**
excerpt:
**Concerns Raised Over Mosque Construction and Church Closures in Rochdale**

Residents in Rochdale have expressed mixed feelings regarding the construction of new mosques in the area, with some voici...
content_html:
**Concerns Raised Over Mosque Construction and Church Closures in Rochdale**

Residents in Rochdale have expressed mixed feelings regarding the construction of new mosques in the area, with some voicing concerns over the closure and demolition of local churches. One community member highlighted that churches were historically funded by taxpayer money and urged those who wish to preserve these places of worship to engage actively by attending services and contributing financially. The discussion reflects broader community sentiments about the evolving religious landscape in Rochdale.

title: **Community Responds to Misconceptions About Local Mosque**
excerpt:
**Community Responds to Misconceptions About Local Mosque**

Rochdale – Siobhain Cunnane has urged community members to read an article regarding the local mosque before commenting on the matter. In h...
content_html:
**Community Responds to Misconceptions About Local Mosque**

Rochdale – Siobhain Cunnane has urged community members to read an article regarding the local mosque before commenting on the matter. In her post, she clarified that the mosque is already established, is not funded by taxpayers, and is unrelated to concerns about farmers, old mill sites, housing, or local retail. Cunnane's remarks come amid ongoing discussions within the community, emphasising the importance of informed dialogue.

Serenity Soul, a wellness centre in the community, has announced a special initiative aimed at providing parents with much-needed relaxation time. The centre invites parents to take a break from their daily responsibilities and enjoy a moment of tranquillity. Further details about the offerings and scheduling have yet to be released. Stay tuned for updates on this opportunity for self-care in the community.

title: **Inquiry Raised on Parent Pay Code for Kingsway Park School**
excerpt:
**Inquiry Raised on Parent Pay Code for Kingsway Park School**

Rochdale, UK – A query has been posted on social media regarding the Parent Pay code for Kingsway Park School. Louise Merriman, a commun...
content_html:
**Inquiry Raised on Parent Pay Code for Kingsway Park School**

Rochdale, UK – A query has been posted on social media regarding the Parent Pay code for Kingsway Park School. Louise Merriman, a community member, asked if the school's administration would provide the code tomorrow.

In response, Sarah Barrett, an admin of the Kingsway Park School Parent Group, suggested that Merriman inquire within the group for further assistance.

The Parent Pay system is an online service that allows parents to make payments for school-related expenses.

Further updates on this matter are awaited from Kingsway Park School.

title: **Burst Water Main Causes Disruption on Shawfield Lane**
excerpt:
**Burst Water Main Causes Disruption on Shawfield Lane**

Rochdale, UK - A burst water main on Shawfield Lane has led to disruptions in the area, with reports indicating that residents in the vicinity...
content_html:
**Burst Water Main Causes Disruption on Shawfield Lane**

Rochdale, UK - A burst water main on Shawfield Lane has led to disruptions in the area, with reports indicating that residents in the vicinity are experiencing a lack of water supply.

Local resident Rachael Pattie reported the incident shortly after it occurred, highlighting that repairs had only recently been completed on the same section of road earlier that afternoon. Another resident, Christine McHugh, confirmed that several homes in the nearby 7 Acres area are affected by the outage.

Residents are urged to check for updates and remain informed as local authorities work to resolve the issue. Further details on the repair timeline and service restoration are yet to be announced.

content_html:
**Local Residents Express Concern Over Road Repairs**

Rochdale, UK – Residents have raised concerns following recent roadworks in the area, as a pothole that was filled earlier today has reportedly reopened. The issue was highlighted by local resident Joanne Burt, who noted that the hole had only just been repaired this afternoon. Community members are now calling for more effective maintenance of the roads to prevent similar incidents in the future.

content_html:
**Local Job Seeker in Bacup Area Seeking Part-Time Employment**

A local resident, Megan Courtney McDade, has expressed her search for a part-time job, aiming for approximately 25 hours of work per week. McDade has experience in various roles, including bar work, front-of-house (FOH) positions, and reception duties. She is looking for opportunities in the Bacup, Burnley, and Rawtenstall areas. Interested parties can reach out to McDade through local community channels.

title: **Community Members Recommend Local Jewellers for Custom Pendants**
excerpt:

Rochdale, UK - Residents of Rochdale have turned to a community Facebook group seeking recommendations for jewellers who can melt d...
content_html:
**Community Members Recommend Local Jewellers for Custom Pendants**

Rochdale, UK - Residents of Rochdale have turned to a community Facebook group seeking recommendations for jewellers who can melt down gold and create custom pendants.

One member suggested a jeweller located at the back of the market in Rochdale, while another resident recommended Harvey’s in Middleton, noting the establishment's long-standing reputation of over 45 years and the exemplary service provided by its proprietor.

Community engagement continues as locals share their experiences and suggestions for custom jewellery services.

content_html:
**Local Jeweller Celebrates Over 45 Years of Service**

Middleton – Tracy Duncan, a resident of the area, has highlighted the longstanding reputation of Harvey’s Jewellers in Middleton, which has been serving customers for over 45 years. Known for its commitment to quality craftsmanship, the shop is praised for its exemplary customer service. Duncan expressed her appreciation for the jeweller, describing the owner as a true gentleman.

title: Arrests after late-night burglary in Heywood
excerpt: Police made two arrests after reports of a late-night burglary near York Street, Heywood.
content_html: Greater Manchester Police confirmed two suspects were detained after officers responded to reports of a break-in near York Street just after midnight. No injuries were reported and enquiries are ongoing.
quotes: Saw flashing lights at around 12:15am – brilliant response time.
Glad no one was hurt. Thank you GMP.

title: Rochdale town centre roadworks: weekend closures announced
excerpt: Temporary closures will be in place this weekend to resurface parts of Yorkshire Street.
content_html: Rochdale Borough Council says diversions will be signposted and buses will continue to run. Drivers are advised to allow extra travel time.
quotes: About time those potholes were fixed!
Please keep access open for businesses.

title: Littleborough school fete raises £4,200 for new library
excerpt: Parents and pupils celebrated a successful summer fete supporting the library project.
content_html: Headteacher Ms. Khan said the funds will go towards books and reading spaces open to the community after school hours.
quotes: The kids can’t wait for the new reading nook!

title: Appeal: witnesses after collision in Milnrow
excerpt: Police are appealing for dashcam footage after a collision on Newhey Road, Milnrow.
content_html: Any witnesses are asked to contact 101 quoting log 2178 of today’s date.
quotes: I drove past just after it happened.

title: Councillors back motion to expand town centre CCTV
excerpt: The council supported a plan to extend CCTV coverage across key town centre spots.
content_html: The motion passed 27-14. Installation is expected later this year, pending procurement.
quotes: Good step for safety if used responsibly.
